content:
Hi, I am writing the tty0tty module in rust right now, and in the process I came across the 'tty_alloc_driver' function, which is a macro function, defined as follows:

#define tty_alloc_driver(lines, flags) \
        __tty_alloc_driver(lines, THIS_MODULE, flags)

bindgen does not handle macros and inline functions, so far I know how to use bindgen to handle inline functions, as follows: 

static inline void tty_set_operations(struct tty_driver *driver,
        const struct tty_operations *op)
{
    driver->ops = op;
}

in the rust/helper.c, i can add this to handle inline function:

//here is for tty0tty
void rust_helper_tty_set_operations(struct tty_driver *driver,const struct tty_operations *op)
{
    return tty_set_operations(driver,op);
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(rust_helper_tty_set_operations);

But I still have some trouble with macro functions, can you tell me how to deal with macro functions?
is there anybody know how to use C side macro function in Rust for Linux?
Bindgen will get basic macros but not yet function macros
You just need to reimplement or call the function directly
Take a look at other similar macros in the source tree
So unlike inline function, we can't use macrofunctions by adding something to helper.c, right?

But I still have some trouble with macro functions, can you tell me how to deal with macro functions?

Can you clarify this question?
You can't really use THIS_MODULE from helpers.c, since that is a simple wrapper function it should be okay to call directly

well, for inline function such as this:

static inline void tty_set_operations(struct tty_driver *driver,
        const struct tty_operations *op)
{
    driver->ops = op;
}

the bindgen can't generate it, and i can't use this function in my rust module. after i add the following thing to helper.c

//here is for tty0tty
void rust_helper_tty_set_operations(struct tty_driver *driver,const struct tty_operations *op)
{
    return tty_set_operations(driver,op);
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(rust_helper_tty_set_operations);

i can find the following thing in rust/bindings/bindings_helpers_generated.rs

extern "C" {
    #[link_name="rust_helper_tty_set_operations"]
    pub fn tty_set_operations(driver: *mut tty_driver, op: *const tty_operations);
}

and the i can use the "tty_set_operations"function in my rust module:

bindings::tty_set_operations(tty_driver_mut_ptr,&SERIAL_OPS);

i though we can do same thing for macro function, bindgen alse can't generate them.
Look at the macro expansion - it calls THIS_MODULE. That won’t work in helpers.c
You should try it just to see the exact errors it gives you

What if he didn't use "THIS_MODULE"? I mean, I just don't know what to add to helper.c so that bindgen can generate the corresponding macros function just like the inline function. Maybe I gave a bad example “tty_alloc_driver” :(

You don’t need to actually add anything to helpers.c; you have to look at how the macro is implemented and reimplement it as a Rust function. That part is easy, it’s is only one call to __tty_alloc_driver.

I’m just suggesting you try adding a helper that wraps the macro tty_alloc_driver as an experiment, not as the solution. It will give you errors when you try to build it, but seeing what the errors are might give you a better understanding of what is going on. (Unless maybe you tried this already)

Ok, I get it, that's what I did, and it worked fine, calling __tty_alloc_driver successfully. I'm just curious if I can modify helper.c or something to use tty_alloc_driver macro directly. Because I might want to develop some automation tools to address bindgen's inability to handle inline functions and macros.
Check the bindgen repo, there is discussion about handling functionlike macros and I think some open PRs
ok, thanks for your advices :)

But that wouldn’t necessarily help here, THIS_MODULE is the bigger problem. Iirc THIS_MODULE creates an undefined symbol that gets set by insmod when you install the module.

…but as I am saying that, I’m second guessing what I said about it not working in helpers.c. I was thinking that there was a problem with linking, but the linker shouldn’t actually care about the undefined symbol - it should wind up in the module’s ko just fine. Unless there is some other magic that I am forgetting.

So you should try making a C function wrapper around the macro in helpers.c and report back with what happens :)

well, again. i meet a problem about the macro function. in the tty0tty.c, it has the following operation:

set_current_state(TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE);

which is a macro define as following:

#define set_current_state(state_value)                  \
    do {                                \
        debug_normal_state_change((state_value));       \
        smp_store_mb(current->__state, (state_value));      \
    } while (0)

how can i use it in rust?
can i use it though bindgen or rust/helper.c?
You can use rust/helpers.c
could you show me an example :) or is there any example how to handle macro in rust/helpers.c
This patch adds some helpers: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20240606-tracepoint-v1-3-6551627bf51b@google.com/
